headline: Is Exxon Mobil (XOM) Pricing Reflect Its 68% One Year Surge And Cash Flow Outlook
summary: If you are wondering whether Exxon Mobil's current share price fairly reflects its long term potential, you are not alone. This article will focus squarely on what you are paying versus what you may be getting. Exxon Mobil shares recently closed at US$163.91, with a 7 day return of a 3.4% decline, a 30 day return of 8.4%, a year to date return of 33.6% and a 1 year return of 68.2% that may change how you think about both upside and risk. These moves have been set against ongoing attention on...

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Noble (NE) Joining Exxon and Halliburton in Fully Automated Offshore Drilling
summary: Halliburton announced that, together with ExxonMobil, Noble and the Wells Alliance Guyana team, it achieved the first fully automated geological well placement with complete rig automation offshore Guyana, integrating rig systems, real-time hydraulics, and subsurface interpretation. This milestone showcases how Noble’s participation in advanced rig automation projects could influence its operational capabilities and role in next‑generation offshore drilling. headline: Exxon Warns of 6% Production Hit From War in First Quarter
summary: Exxon Mobil expects the Iran war to curtail its global oil-and-gas production by 6% in the first quarter, after the closure of a key Middle East waterway and attacks on gas assets in March.  The U.S. oil giant’s assets in Qatar and the United Arab Emirates make up about 20% of the company’s worldwide output, “but a smaller percentage of upstream earnings,” it said Wednesday in a preview of its first-quarter earnings due in May. Exxon pumped 4.7 million barrels of oil and gas per day in the fourth quarter.  In the first quarter, Exxon said it expected a boost to earnings of up to about $2.9 billion from higher oil and natural-gas prices, compared with the prior quarter, when it reported a $6.5 billion profit.

Exxon pumped 4.7 million barrels of oil and gas per day in the fourth quarter.  In the first quarter, Exxon said it expected a boost to earnings of up to about $2.9 billion from higher oil and natural-gas prices, compared with the prior quarter, when it reported a $6.5 billion profit.

headline: Exxon Sees 6% of Its Worldwide Output Shut on Mideast Conflict
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Exxon Mobil Corp. said 6% of its global first-quarter production was knocked out as the Iran war paralyzed much of the Persian Gulf energy industry.Half of those outages were concentrated at a liquefied natural gas complex in Qatar in which Exxon is a partner, the company said on Wednesday. Two LNG production lines, or trains, were damaged.“Public reports indicate the damage will take a prolonged period to repair,” Exxon said in a statement. “Pending an on-site evaluation, we are

headline: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

content.title: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
content.description:
Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.

Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.

Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.
content.summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

headline: Exxon Sees First-Quarter Production Loss of 6% on Iran War
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Exxon Mobil Corp. lost 6% of its global production in the first quarter as the Iran war paralyzed oil and natural gas operations in the Persian Gulf.The Strait of Hormuz’s closure over the past five weeks have severely affected Exxon’s operations in the United Arab Emirates and Qatar, where it owns stakes in the giant Las Raffan liquefied natural gas complex, the company said in a statement Wednesday. Those two nations account for about 20% of Exxon’s global annual production. Exx

headline: Is the Drop in Energy Stocks a Buying Opportunity? What Chevron’s Chart Says.
summary: Energy stocks are sliding today, but the pullback may be temporary.  The recently-announced cease-fire in the Middle East has eased immediate geopolitical concerns, removing the primary driver of higher crude prices.  Chevron  the ETF’s second-largest component at 17% of assets, looks attractive as it now trades 12% below its most recent 52-week high.

content.title: Is the Drop in Energy Stocks a Buying Opportunity? What Chevron’s Chart Says.
content.summary: Energy stocks are sliding today, but the pullback may be temporary.  The recently-announced cease-fire in the Middle East has eased immediate geopolitical concerns, removing the primary driver of higher crude prices.  Chevron  the ETF’s second-largest component at 17% of assets, looks attractive as it now trades 12% below its most recent 52-week high.
